title: Gouldia ovata
intro: Gouldia ovata, also known as the Guava tree, is a flowering evergreen plant native to New Caledonia, characterized by its small white or yellowish flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect against infections, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gouldia ovata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Gouldia ovata is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is native to New Caledonia, a territory in the Pacific Ocean. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Gouldia, which contains a few species of flowering plants. The classification of Gouldia ovata is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its oval-shaped leaves and white flowers. The specific classification of the plant is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Ericales, Family: Myrsinaceae, Genus: Gouldia, Species: G. ovata.
